title: She knit an MBTA-themed sweater inspired by her commutes. The internet is loving it.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston graphic designer Megan Lam created a hand-knit sweater featuring the MBTA's commuter rail and Orange, Green, and Red lines.
Lam's MBTA-themed sweater was inspired by her personal experiences and previous designs from transit-themed creators.
The sweater gained attention on Instagram, highlighting a growing interest in unique, creative fiber arts projects.

title: Wellesley high school senior to run first Boston Marathon after years on Heartbreak Hill
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Emmie Laudano, an 18-year-old Wellesley senior, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for the first time.
Laudano has trained on Heartbreak Hill as part of the Dreamfar program, preparing for the 26.2-mile race.
She is raising funds for the Tierney Learning Center to support educational resources for Boston's youth.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a news quiz to test knowledge of recent Greater Boston stories.
The quiz is designed to help readers stay informed about local trending news.
Peter Chianca, a general assignment editor, has been with Boston.com since 2019.

title: Marco Sturm: Bruins ‘didn’t respect’ Panthers in frustrating loss
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins head coach Marco Sturm criticized his team for underestimating a shorthanded Florida Panthers in their 2-1 loss.
The Bruins' lone goal was scored by Fraser Minten in the first period, but Sergei Bobrovsky stopped 14 third-period shots.
Despite recent performance downturns against weaker teams, the Bruins retain a 95% playoff probability according to MoneyPuck.

title: Mass. man sentenced for illegally trafficking dozens of endangered animal parts
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Adam Bied from Reading, Mass. was sentenced to eight months in prison for trafficking over 100 endangered animal parts.
Bied pleaded guilty to smuggling goods and violating the Lacey Act, with parts from threatened species like orangutans and leopards.
Bied's illegally imported wildlife parts were falsely labeled, and he was caught selling leopard skulls to an undercover federal agent.

title: Taunton man convicted in fatal stabbing of Falmouth 19-year-old
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Adrian Black, from Taunton, was convicted of first-degree murder for the 2023 fatal stabbing of Milteer Hendricks, 19.
The attack occurred outside an East Falmouth apartment complex, where Black and others planned to confront Hendricks.
Hendricks died after being med-flighted to Boston; Black faces a mandatory life sentence without parole.

title: State Police lieutenant named in report about sergeant’s alleged drunk driving reportedly suspended
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Lt. Anthony DeLucia was suspended with pay after being named in an investigative report by Middlesex DA.
The report involves Sgt. Scott Quigley's alleged drunk driving crash that resulted in a fatality in December 2023.
Details of the crash became public after allegations arose during a Lowell murder trial Quigley was investigating.

title: Mass. couple sentenced to prison for manslaughter in death of infant foster child
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Tucker and Cassandra Barlow-Tucker received three to five years in prison for manslaughter of their 10-month-old foster child.
The infant died from treatable infections due to neglect; the couple ignored clear signs the child needed medical treatment.
Barlow-Tucker and Tucker are prohibited from fostering or caregiving for children other than their own in the future.

title: Salisbury woman gets life without parole for poisoning boyfriend
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Judy Church, 67, was sentenced to life without parole for poisoning her boyfriend's Powerade with antifreeze.
Church was convicted of first-degree murder for the 2022 death of Leroy Fowler due to ethylene glycol poisoning.
Church filmed Fowler in distress and previously joked about poisoning him due to jealousy over his affair.

title: 5 concerning trends from the Red Sox’ ugly start to 2026 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Red Sox have started the 2026 season with a 1-5 record, their worst start since 2019.
Boston's pitching staff struggles, currently ranking 26th in team ERA and last in home runs allowed per nine innings.
Key offensive players, including Roman Anthony and Trevor Story, are underperforming with low batting averages and high strikeouts.
